description: Sprint 2026-04-02: B-028 Phase 1 Alert Thresholds (Spool Tuning Spec), B-032 PID Polling Validation + Data Architecture

userStories.title: Implement Coolant Temperature Alert Thresholds
userStories.description: As a driver, I want the system to alert me at specific coolant temperature ranges so that I can prevent head gasket failure. The 4G63 MLS head gasket loses clamp force when head bolts stretch from heat. Above 220F, the gasket weeps coolant into #4 cylinder. This is the most common 4G63 failure mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Coolant temp thresholds implemented with 4 levels in config and alert system
Cold: < 160F — Blue indicator (info only). Engine not at operating temp. Normal during warmup.
Normal: 180-210F — Green. No action.
Caution: 210-220F — Yellow, audible beep. Alert: 'Coolant temperature elevated ({value}F). Monitor closely.'
Danger: > 220F — Red, continuous alert. Alert: 'DANGER: Coolant temperature critical ({value}F). Risk of head gasket failure. Reduce load immediately.'
Thresholds sourced from obd_config.json (not hardcoded) so they can be adjusted
Alert log entry created at Caution and Danger transitions with severity and message
Tests validate each threshold boundary: 159F=cold, 160F=cold, 179F=cold, 180F=normal, 210F=normal, 211F=caution, 220F=caution, 221F=danger
Tests validate alert log entries match expected format from Spool spec
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 29 tests, all passing. 4-level tiered thresholds (Cold/Normal/Caution/Danger) in src/alert/tiered_thresholds.py. Config-driven via obd_config.json tieredThresholds.coolantTemp. Alert log entries at Caution/Danger with Spool-spec messages. All boundary tests pass: 159F=cold, 160F=cold, 179F=cold, 180F=normal, 210F=normal, 211F=caution, 220F=caution, 221F=danger. Full regression 968 passed.

userStories.title: Implement STFT Alert Thresholds
userStories.description: As a driver, I want the system to alert me when STFT values are abnormal so that I can detect fueling problems. STFT shows what the ECU is doing RIGHT NOW to correct fueling. Large positive STFT means the engine is running lean and the ECU is adding fuel to compensate. On a turbocharged engine, lean = detonation = dead engine.
acceptanceCriteria: STFT thresholds implemented with 3 levels in config and alert system
Normal: -5% to +5% — ECU making small corrections. Healthy.
Caution: -10% to -5% OR +5% to +10% — ECU working harder. Log for trend analysis.
Danger: < -15% OR > +15% — ECU at correction limit. Active fueling problem. If positive (lean): could be vacuum leak, weak fuel pump, or clogged injector.
Thresholds sourced from obd_config.json (not hardcoded)
Alert messages distinguish positive (lean) from negative (rich) deviations
Tests validate symmetric boundaries: -15.1=danger, -15=danger, -10=caution, -5=caution, -4.9=normal, +4.9=normal, +5=caution, +10=caution, +15=danger, +15.1=danger
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 32 tests, all passing. 3-level symmetric thresholds (Normal/Caution/Danger) using abs(value) with lean/rich message distinction. STFTThresholds dataclass + evaluateSTFT + loadSTFTThresholds in src/alert/tiered_thresholds.py. Config-driven via obd_config.json tieredThresholds.stft. All boundary tests pass: -15.1=danger, -15=danger, -10=caution, -5=caution, -4.9=normal, +4.9=normal, +5=caution, +10=caution, +15=danger, +15.1=danger. Full regression 1000 passed.

userStories.title: Implement Timing Advance Alert Thresholds
userStories.description: As a driver, I want the system to detect timing retard events so that I can identify possible knock/detonation. Without ECMLink, timing advance is our only window into knock activity. The stock ECU retards timing when the knock sensor detects detonation. A sudden drop at a specific RPM/load means the engine is knocking there.
acceptanceCriteria: Timing advance thresholds implemented with 3 levels in config and alert system
Normal: Matches baseline for RPM/load point. ECU is happy with combustion.
Caution: Drops > 5 degrees below baseline suddenly. Possible knock event. Log it. If repeated at same RPM/load, flag for review.
Danger: Goes to 0 or negative under load. Active detonation. Alert: 'Reduce throttle immediately. Do not continue at this load.'
System tracks a per-RPM/load baseline (from initial drives or configurable defaults)
Sudden drops detected relative to baseline, not absolute values
Repeated timing pulls at the same RPM/load point flagged as a pattern
Thresholds sourced from obd_config.json
Tests validate baseline comparison logic and sudden-drop detection
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 38 tests, all passing. 3-level baseline-relative system (Normal/Caution/Danger) in src/alert/timing_thresholds.py. TimingRetardTracker class tracks per-RPM/load baselines (500 RPM x 10% load bands) with running average learning. Caution: drop > 5° below baseline. Danger: value <= 0° under load (>30%). Pattern detection flags repeated retards at same operating point (default 3 occurrences). Config-driven via obd_config.json tieredThresholds.timingAdvance. Baseline learning only during normal operation (retarded values don't corrupt baselines). Full regression 1038 passed.

userStories.title: Implement Engine RPM Alert Thresholds
userStories.description: As a driver, I want RPM boundary alerts so that I can avoid over-revving and detect idle issues.
acceptanceCriteria: RPM thresholds implemented with 4 levels in config and alert system
Low idle: < 600 RPM — Idle too low. Possible vacuum leak or IAC issue.
Normal: 600-6500 RPM — No action.
Caution: 6500-7000 RPM — High RPM warning. Stock redline approaching.
Danger: > 7200 RPM — Over-rev. Valve float risk on stock springs.
Thresholds sourced from obd_config.json (not hardcoded)
Tests validate all boundaries: 599=low_idle, 600=normal, 6500=normal, 6501=caution, 7000=caution, 7200=caution, 7201=danger
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 28 tests, all passing. 4-level tiered thresholds (Low Idle/Normal/Caution/Danger) in src/alert/tiered_thresholds.py. RPMThresholds dataclass + evaluateRPM + loadRPMThresholds. Config-driven via obd_config.json tieredThresholds.rpm. Low idle (<600) flags vacuum leak/IAC. Caution (>6500) warns redline approaching. Danger (>7200) alerts valve float risk. All boundary tests pass: 599=low_idle, 600=normal, 6500=normal, 6501=caution, 7000=caution, 7200=caution, 7201=danger. Full regression 1066 passed.

userStories.title: Implement Battery Voltage Alert Thresholds
userStories.description: As a driver, I want charging system alerts so that I can detect alternator or regulator failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Battery voltage thresholds implemented with 3 levels in config and alert system (engine running)
Normal: 13.5-14.5V — Charging system healthy.
Caution: 12.5-13.5V OR 14.5-14.8V — Low = weak alternator. High = regulator starting to fail.
Danger: < 12.0V OR > 15.0V — Low = charging failure, engine may stall. High = regulator failed, will cook battery and electronics.
PID source: 0x42 (Control Module Voltage) or hardware UPS reading
Thresholds sourced from obd_config.json (not hardcoded)
Alert messages distinguish low-voltage from high-voltage conditions
Tests validate both low and high boundary conditions
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 28 tests, all passing. 3-level bidirectional thresholds (Normal/Caution/Danger) with low-voltage and high-voltage message distinction. BatteryVoltageThresholds dataclass + evaluateBatteryVoltage + loadBatteryVoltageThresholds in src/alert/tiered_thresholds.py. Config-driven via obd_config.json tieredThresholds.batteryVoltage. Normal: 13.5-14.5V. Caution low: weak alternator (<13.5V). Caution high: regulator starting to fail (>14.5V). Danger low: charging failure (<=12.0V). Danger high: regulator failed (>15.0V). All boundary tests pass. Full regression 1094 passed.

userStories.title: Implement Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Alert Thresholds
userStories.description: As a driver, I want IAT alerts so that I can detect heat soak and knock risk conditions. The IAT sensor is inside the MAF housing on 2G DSMs — constant -40F reading means sensor disconnected or failed (known 2G quirk).
acceptanceCriteria: IAT thresholds implemented with 4 levels in config and alert system
Normal: Ambient to 130F — Normal for turbocharged intake.
Caution: 130-150F — Heat soak building. Power loss. Increased knock risk.
Danger: > 160F — Significant knock risk at boost. Reduce load.
Sensor Failure: Fixed at -40F (-40C) — IAT sensor disconnected or failed (known 2G quirk, sensor is inside MAF housing).
Sensor failure detection: if IAT reads exactly -40F for N consecutive readings (configurable, default 5), flag as sensor failure not cold air
Thresholds sourced from obd_config.json (not hardcoded)
Tests validate boundaries and sensor failure detection
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 31 tests, all passing. 4-level system (Normal/Caution/Danger/Sensor Failure) in src/alert/iat_thresholds.py. IATThresholds dataclass + evaluateIAT (stateless) + IATSensorTracker (stateful consecutive -40F detection). Normal: <=130F. Caution: >130F-160F (heat soak, knock risk). Danger: >160F (significant knock risk at boost). Sensor failure: -40F for N consecutive readings (configurable, default 5) — known 2G DSM quirk (sensor in MAF housing). Config-driven via obd_config.json tieredThresholds.iat. All boundary tests pass. Full regression 1125 passed.

userStories.title: Validate Phase 1 Tiered Polling Configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the polling tiers to match Spool's tuning-driven priorities so that safety-critical parameters are always fresh. The system already has a tiered polling concept; this validates and updates it to match Spool's specification.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify/update polling config to match Spool's 4-tier structure
Tier 1 — Every cycle (1 Hz): PID 0x05 Coolant Temp, PID 0x0C RPM, PID 0x04 Engine Load, PID 0x0E Timing Advance, PID 0x06 STFT B1
Tier 2 — Every 3 cycles (~0.3 Hz): PID 0x11 Throttle Position, PID 0x0D Vehicle Speed
Tier 3 — Every 10 cycles (~0.1 Hz): PID 0x07 LTFT B1, PID 0x0F Intake Air Temp, PID 0x14 O2 Sensor B1S1
Tier 4 — Every 30 cycles (~0.03 Hz): PID 0x42 Control Module Voltage, PID 0x0B Manifold Pressure (MDP)
MDP caveat documented: PID 0x0B is MDP (EGR only) on 2G Eclipse, NOT true manifold pressure — cannot be used for boost measurement
Config file (obd_config.json) reflects all 4 tiers with correct cycle counts
Tests validate polling frequency matches tier assignments
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 72 tests, all passing. 4-tier polling structure in obd_config.json pollingTiers section matching Spool's spec. Tier 1 (cycle=1): COOLANT_TEMP, RPM, ENGINE_LOAD, TIMING_ADVANCE, SHORT_FUEL_TRIM_1. Tier 2 (cycle=3): THROTTLE_POS, SPEED. Tier 3 (cycle=10): LONG_FUEL_TRIM_1, INTAKE_TEMP, O2_B1S1. Tier 4 (cycle=30): CONTROL_MODULE_VOLTAGE, INTAKE_PRESSURE. MDP caveat documented on INTAKE_PRESSURE (PID 0x0B is EGR only on 2G Eclipse). CONTROL_MODULE_VOLTAGE added to realtimeData parameters. Loader in src/obd/data/polling_tiers.py with PollingTierConfig, shouldPollParameter, getParametersForCycle. Full regression 1197 passed.

userStories.title: Define ECMLink Parameter Ingestion Schema (Phase 2)
userStories.description: As an architect, I want the database schema designed for ECMLink data so that Phase 2 integration is straightforward when hardware is ready. ECMLink provides serial data at 10-50 Hz with 50+ parameters — completely different data volume from OBD-II.
acceptanceCriteria: ECMLink parameter schema defined for 15 priority parameters: Wideband AFR (float, 20Hz), Knock Count (int, 20Hz), Knock Sum (int, 20Hz), Boost/MAP (float, 20Hz), Injector Duty Cycle (float, 10Hz), Timing Advance (float, 20Hz), RPM (int, 20Hz), TPS (float, 20Hz), Coolant Temp (float, 5Hz), IAT (float, 5Hz), Ethanol Content (float, 1Hz), Target AFR (float, 10Hz), STFT (float, 10Hz), LTFT (float, 1Hz), Barometric Pressure (float, 0.5Hz)
Database table(s) designed to handle mixed sample rates (20Hz, 10Hz, 5Hz, 1Hz, 0.5Hz)
Schema documented in specs/architecture.md with ECMLink section
Ingestion interface defined: how ECMLink serial data enters the system
Design doc only — no runtime implementation required (blocked on ECMLink hardware)
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Design Data Volume Architecture for Phase 2
userStories.description: As an architect, I want storage and retention strategy designed for Phase 2 data volumes so that the system handles ECMLink's 30x data increase. Phase 1: ~18K rows/hr. Phase 2: ~540K rows/hr. Season estimate: ~21.6M rows on ECMLink.
acceptanceCriteria: Data volume estimates documented: Phase 1 ~5 reads/sec = ~18K rows/hr, Phase 2 ~150 reads/sec = ~540K rows/hr, seasonal ~21.6M rows
Pi SQLite storage strategy documented: disk usage estimate per million rows, can Pi store a full season?
Chi-Srv-01 MariaDB strategy documented: retention policy, partitioning approach, indexing for 21M+ rows/season
Sync bandwidth estimate: time to sync a 2-hour ECMLink drive over WiFi
Architecture decisions documented in specs/architecture.md
Retention policy validated: 90 days on Pi, forever on server — confirm these work at Phase 2 volumes
Design doc only — no runtime implementation required
All existing tests still pass
